section_anchor: Plate and mount acoustics: transmission and isolation
brief_summary: Two-axis grid diagram with plate stiffness (rigid to compliant) on the horizontal axis and mount isolation (coupled to isolated) on the vertical axis. Four quadrants label the predictable acoustic zones: deep thock (gasket-mount + PC/POM plate), controlled stiffness (top-mount + aluminum), bright ring (tray-mount + brass), and soft clack (tray-mount + PC). The deep thock quadrant is washed in ochre as the focal zone.
data_sources: apps/web/src/content/articles/keyboard-acoustics-deep-dive.mdx:Plate and mount acoustics — 'A brass plate in a rigid top-mount rings loudly and decays slowly; the same brass plate in a well-tuned gasket-mount sounds controlled and meaty.'
apps/web/src/content/articles/keyboard-acoustics-deep-dive.mdx:Plate and mount acoustics — 'Picking plate material without specifying mount type is an incomplete conversation.'
apps/web/src/content/articles/plate-materials-explained.mdx:The material walk — 'polycarbonate plate plus gasket-mount plus a thock-forward switch is the deep thock default.'
